text.title: Changelog Automation Conventional Commits Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules every changelog-update artefact must satisfy.

rule.statement: Commits are grouped by canonical type: `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `refactor`, `docs`, `perf`, `test`, `build`, `ci`, `style`. Each group becomes a section in the release block; unknown types are flagged as deviations.
rule.rationale: Stable section names let readers (and tools) parse the changelog deterministically.

rule.statement: Commits marked with `!:` (e.g. `feat!:`) OR containing `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer MUST appear in a `### BREAKING CHANGES` section at the top of the release block, with the per-line description and migration hint.
rule.rationale: Readers scan the top first; burying breakings in feat/fix sections is the principal cause of accidental upgrade breakage.

rule.statement: When a commit declares `<type>(<scope>):`, the scope is preserved as a prefix on the line: `- (api) add /v2/quote endpoint`. Missing scope is allowed for top-level changes.
rule.rationale: Scope tells the reader whether the line concerns them in 1 second.

rule.statement: Merge commits (`Merge branch …`, `Merge pull request …`) are dropped from the changelog. Squash-merge produces a single conventional commit per PR; that is what enters.
rule.rationale: Merge commits add no information; they pollute the changelog with `Merge branch X into Y` lines.

rule.statement: Semver bump is derived deterministically: any breaking → major; feat → minor; fix / chore / docs / test / refactor / perf / build / ci / style → patch. The bump appears as `## [<new_version>] - <date>` heading. Mismatch between declared bump and computed is a hard error.
rule.rationale: A consistent bump rule lets tooling tag releases without human judgment.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (range, prior CHANGELOG, scope inventory), execute: pick-range → parse → group → emit-md → validate. Do not skip the validation; pre-merge gate refuses changelog-less releases.
rule.rationale: Skipped validations let malformed commits land; the changelog becomes unreliable.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) repo does not follow conventional-commits and won't be normalized, (b) monorepo with independent per-package releases — use a changesets-style tool, (c) releases are internal-only with no audience, (d) <5 commits per release — handwritten is faster.
rule.rationale: Automation overhead is wasted when input is not conventional or audience does not consume the artefact.
